stemHtml:
Refer to figure. 
For pre-flight planning purposes, calculate the total fuel required for a Commercial Air Transport flight with a Performance Class A aeroplane, given the following information. Assume the Basic Fuel Scheme WITHOUT variations applies.

The weather at the destination aerodrome is forecast to be below the applicable minima, but two alternates with suitable weather have been chosen. The flight's payload means that minimum block fuel will be taken.

Taxi Fuel: 230 kg 
Trip distance: 250 NGM 
Average Wind Component: 50 kt headwind 
Holding fuel flow: 2400 kg/h 
Expected Landing Mass: 65 000 kg 
Destination Alternate 1 Fuel: 1230 kg 
Destination Alternate 2 Fuel: 1950 kg 
Additional Fuel: 0 kg 
Extra Fuel: 0 kg 
Discretionary Fuel: 0 kg
